techniques.evidence_quote: Through the threat actor's likely rigorous scanning and enumeration phase, they came to the conclusion that attacking a DevSecOps home computer would be their best bet at gaining access to LastPass data. The threat actor found that there were four DevSecOps engineers who had access to the corporate vault containing decryption keys.
the threat actor found that third-party media software running on a DevSecOps engineer's home computer had a fatal vulnerability; once exploited, they would be able to remotely execute code on it.

the software engineer whose corporate laptop was compromised was using an outdated version of the 'Plex Media Server software'. This version of the Plex Media Server software had a known vulnerability, CVE-2020-5741, that the threat actor was able to exploit.
The vulnerabilities in a third-party media app on the engineer's computer allowed for remote code execution, which enabled the attacker to install a keylogger on their device.

With this exploit, the threat actor was able to install a keylogger on the engineer's computer. Using the keylogger, they were able to capture the engineer's master password as it was entered after logging in with multifactor authentication.

some of these source code repositories included cleartext embedded credentials, stored digital certificates related to our development environments, and some encrypted credentials used for production capabilities such as backup.

the threat actor was able to keep their identity unknown and bypass intrusion detection systems thanks to the use of a third-party VPN service. This hid the origin of their activity when accessing the cloud-based development environment.
allowed them to impersonate the software engineer
the threat actor accessed a shared cloud storage environment powered by AWS... By the time LastPass was alerted of this, the threat actor had already extracted customer and vault data.

Rather than attempting to steal lower-level credentials and escalating privileges, gaining access to a DevSecOps engineer's credentials would allow for immediate access to the data they were looking for.
The threat 'engaged in a new series of reconnaissance, enumeration, and exfiltration activities,' which involved LastPass' AWS S3 storage buckets.
the threat actor exfiltrated fourteen code repositories from the cloud-based development environment

The threat used their access to start copying 'five binary large objects database shards'. The exfiltration of the database backups began... LastPass announced that the threat gained access to a third-party cloud-based storage service used to store archived backups of LastPass production data.
the threat actor exfiltrated fourteen code repositories from the cloud-based development environment
Most of the highly sensitive customer data is compromised, excluding the master passwords of users.

attack_chain.description: Threat actor conducted reconnaissance to identify high-value targets within LastPass, determining that four senior DevSecOps engineers had privileged access to the corporate vault containing decryption keys.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor found that there were four DevSecOps engineers who had access to the corporate vault containing decryption keys.

attack_chain.description: Attacker identified that a targeted DevSecOps engineer was running a vulnerable, unpatched version of Plex Media Server (CVE-2020-5741) on their personal home computer.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actor found that third-party media software running on a DevSecOps engineer's home computer had a fatal vulnerability; once exploited, they would be able to remotely execute code on it.

attack_chain.description: Attacker exploited CVE-2020-5741 in the unpatched Plex Media Server running on the DevSecOps engineer's home computer to achieve remote code execution.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the software engineer whose corporate laptop was compromised was using an outdated version of the 'Plex Media Server software'. This version of the Plex Media Server software had a known vulnerability, CVE-2020-5741, that the threat actor was able to exploit.

attack_chain.description: Remote code execution via the Plex vulnerability was used to install a keylogger on the engineer's personal/corporate machine.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The vulnerabilities in a third-party media app on the engineer's computer allowed for remote code execution, which enabled the attacker to install a keylogger on their device.

attack_chain.description: The installed keylogger captured the DevSecOps engineer's master password as it was typed, even after MFA was satisfied, compromising cloud vault credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Using the keylogger, they were able to capture the engineer's master password as it was entered after logging in with multifactor authentication.

attack_chain.description: Using the stolen credentials, the attacker authenticated to LastPass's cloud-based development environment as the compromised engineer, bypassing MFA.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an unidentified person or group had stolen the engineer's credentials to access this environment

attack_chain.description: The attacker used a third-party VPN service to mask their origin and bypass intrusion detection systems while impersonating the engineer in the cloud environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actor was able to keep their identity unknown and bypass intrusion detection systems thanks to the use of a third-party VPN service. This hid the origin of their activity when accessing the cloud-based development environment and allowed them to impersonate the software engineer.

attack_chain.description: From the development environment, the attacker exfiltrated fourteen source code repositories, including cleartext embedded credentials, digital certificates, API keys, and encrypted production credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actor exfiltrated fourteen code repositories from the cloud-based development environment... some of these source code repositories included cleartext embedded credentials, stored digital certificates related to our development environments, and some encrypted credentials used for production capabilities such as backup.

attack_chain.description: Attacker harvested cleartext embedded credentials and API keys found within the exfiltrated source code repositories, enabling further lateral movement toward production infrastructure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: some of these source code repositories included cleartext embedded credentials, stored digital certificates related to our development environments, and some encrypted credentials used for production capabilities such as backup.

attack_chain.description: Using credentials obtained from the development environment, the attacker conducted enumeration of LastPass AWS S3 storage buckets to identify valuable backup and production data targets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat 'engaged in a new series of reconnaissance, enumeration, and exfiltration activities,' which involved LastPass' AWS S3 storage buckets.

attack_chain.description: Attacker copied five BLOB database shards and then accessed a third-party cloud storage service containing archived backups of LastPass production data, exfiltrating encrypted password vaults, MFA database backups, and broad customer PII.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat used their access to start copying 'five binary large objects database shards'. The exfiltration of the database backups began... LastPass announced that the threat gained access to a third-party cloud-based storage service used to store archived backups of LastPass production data. Most of the highly sensitive customer data is compromised.

initial_access_vector: Exploitation of CVE-2020-5741 in unpatched Plex Media Server running on a senior DevSecOps engineer's personal home computer, enabling remote code execution
persistence_mechanism: Attacker used stolen valid cloud credentials (captured via keylogger) with VPN masking to repeatedly access AWS-based development and production environments over multiple months, using compromised IAM user credentials to maintain persistent access
privilege_escalation: Attacker directly targeted a senior DevSecOps engineer who already held privileged access to the corporate vault and decryption keys, bypassing the need for traditional privilege escalation. Credentials harvested from source code repositories then enabled access to production/backup infrastructure.
